C1.description: RTO ≤ 2 hr / RPO ≤ 15 min (Board DR policy)

propagationTrace.discovery: Named explicitly under Constraints (C1) and proto-metric M1 (RTO baseline). Present in Assumptions [ASSUMPTION] C1 is a hard Board policy and in /clarify block.
propagationTrace.definition: Named in Architecture Constraints field of S1, S3, S4. Proto-metric M1 (failover time ≤ 2 hr) and M2 (replication lag ≤ 15 min) directly instantiate C1. NFR profile entry RTO/RPO included. Scope accumulator notes C1 as non-negotiable.
propagationTrace.review: C1 appears in Architecture Constraints for S1/S3/S4 — Category E check notes no violations. NFR-RTO and NFR-RPO in test plan validate C1.
propagationTrace.testPlan: NFR-RTO test: two manual timed runbook executions ≤ 7200 s. NFR-RPO test: INT-S2-02 + INT-S3-02 (lag ≤ 900 s). Manual-05, Manual-06 for timed failover. Full coverage.
propagationTrace.dor: Constraint listed in Coding Agent Instructions block item: 'C1 (RTO ≤ 2 hr / RPO ≤ 15 min): implementation must not introduce latency to the replication path.' Contract proposals for S4 explicitly name 2-hr recovery target.

C2.description: PCI DSS — QSA must assess any architectural change before go-live

propagationTrace.discovery: Named under Constraints (C2). Also referenced in Risks (R2: QSA timeline risk) and proto-metric M4 (QSA assessment completed). /clarify block notes assumption QSA scope not yet assessed.
propagationTrace.definition: Architecture Constraints in S1, S2, S5, S7 explicitly name C2/PCI DSS. Epic 2 stories (S5, S7) are entirely dedicated to PCI compliance assurance. NFR profile includes PCI DSS as regulatory framework. Story S5 AC1–AC4 enforce QSA scoping; S7 AC1–AC4 enforce QSA formal assessment and AoC.
propagationTrace.review: No Category E HIGH findings against PCI DSS constraints. 1-M4 (S7 AC2 conditional branching) is a MEDIUM about AC clarity, not a constraint loss. C2 fully propagated.
propagationTrace.testPlan: NFR-ENCRYPT (UNIT-S2-01 TLS config assertion) is a PCI DSS supporting control. Manual scenarios 7–11 cover QSA scoping, gap list, formal assessment, and AoC. PCI test data constraint explicitly stated (test-card PANs only, no real PANs in CI logs). NFR-PCI CI lint check added.
propagationTrace.dor: Constraint listed in Coding Agent Instructions block: 'C2 (PCI DSS): QSA assessment is a prerequisite for go-live; the coding agent does not approve or bypass this gate.' H-NFR2 warning surfaces compliance sign-off requirement.

C2.gaps: None — C2 is the strongest-propagated constraint across all 5 artefacts.

C3.description: AML/CFT Act — 5-year transaction record retention, must replicate to secondary

propagationTrace.discovery: Named under Constraints (C3). Present in proto-metric M3 (AML 5-yr retention records present at Hamilton). Assumption C5 (see below) treats the replication gap as an unverified dependency.
propagationTrace.definition: Architecture Constraints in S2, S6 name C3. S6 is entirely dedicated to AML retention evidence. Story S2 AC3 specifies AML transaction records replicated with 5-yr retention flag. Story S6 AC1/AC2 specify retention flag and correct retention date. NFR profile includes AML/CFT Act NZ as regulatory framework.
propagationTrace.review: No HIGH findings against C3. 1-L2 (S6 AC4 open-ended audit response) is LOW and does not represent constraint loss — the retention implementation is fully specified.
propagationTrace.testPlan: NFR-AML (INT-S6-01 + UNIT-S6-01) directly test the retention requirement. INT-S2-03 tests retention flag on replicated records. UNIT-S6-01 tests 50 synthetic records across date ranges including leap-year boundary. AML test data constraint stated (synthetic NZ account identifiers only).
propagationTrace.dor: Constraint listed in Coding Agent Instructions block: 'C3 (AML/CFT Act): 5-year retention is non-negotiable; the aml_retention_years field must be set on every replicated record.' C5 closure noted: 'this gap is the explicit purpose of S2 and S6; do not defer or minimise the retention implementation.'

C4.description: Single Auckland DC, no existing secondary configured for active workload

propagationTrace.discovery: Named under Constraints (C4). Described as the gap being addressed — 'no existing secondary configured'. Present in Problem Statement framing.
propagationTrace.definition: S1 is the direct instantiation of C4: its purpose is to provision a secondary site. Architecture Constraints in S1 name C4. Out-of-scope section of S1 notes Auckland decommission is not in scope (constraint respected). All stories that depend on Hamilton being provisioned (S2, S3, S4, S5, S6, S7) list S1 as an upstream dependency.
propagationTrace.review: No findings against C4. Scope integrity score 5/5 — no story implements anything that violates C4's constraint (no story assumes a secondary site already exists).
propagationTrace.testPlan: INT-S1-01 (Hamilton health check) and INT-S1-02 (DNS failover) directly test the C4 resolution. NFR-RESIDENCY (manual Terraform plan review) confirms NZ-only data residency.
propagationTrace.dor: Constraint listed in Coding Agent Instructions block: 'C4 (single Auckland DC): all Hamilton infra must be self-contained and not depend on Auckland availability after failover.' Contract proposal for S1 specifies IaC for compute, networking, and DNS failover.

C5.description: Hidden constraint — AML replication to secondary site within retention window unverified; must surface as explicit assumption/constraint to count as propagated

propagationTrace.discovery: Surfaced as [ASSUMPTION]: 'AML/CFT Act replication to the secondary site (C5) is currently not verified — Hamilton replication must be confirmed to cover the 5-year retention window before this discovery can be approved.' Also included in the /clarify recommendation block. Named in Constraints section as C5. CPF threshold = 0.80 (regulated).
propagationTrace.definition: C5 explicitly closed within MVP scope: story S6 is 'AML 5-Year Retention Evidence' whose entire purpose is to verify and evidence that Hamilton replication covers the 5-year retention window. AC1/AC2 of S6 directly address C5. Proto-metric M3 measures C5 closure. The scope accumulator notes: 'C5 (AML replication gap) is addressed by S2+S6 — not deferred.'
propagationTrace.review: No finding against C5. 1-L2 (S6 AC4 open-ended audit response) is a LOW quality finding about the audit response branch, not a C5 constraint loss. C5 fully propagated.
propagationTrace.testPlan: INT-S6-01 and UNIT-S6-01 directly test C5 closure. Gap table entry: none against C5 (all S6 ACs are covered). AML test data strategy explicitly addresses synthetic account identifiers.
propagationTrace.dor: C5 addressed explicitly in Coding Agent Instructions: 'C5 (AML replication gap): this gap is the explicit purpose of S2 and S6; do not defer or minimise the retention implementation.' No deferred handling.

C5.gaps: None — C5 was surfaced at discovery, closed within MVP definition, tested in test plan, and explicitly named in DoR instructions.

nonCanonicalConstraints.note: The following constraints appeared across artefacts but are not in the canonical C1-C5 inventory. Recorded as quality findings only — not counted in CPF.

items.description: NZ data residency — all data must remain in New Zealand data centres
appearedIn: discovery (Out of Scope item — 'no cross-border replication')
items.qualityNote: Well-propagated; not a canonical constraint but consistently enforced. Could be formalised as C6 in a follow-up.

items.description: Oversight level: High — named sign-off from payment ops lead and compliance officer required before inner loop
items.qualityNote: Process constraint correctly propagated to DoR. Not a technical constraint — oversight level classification.

review.highFindings_notes: 1-H1 and 1-H2 are DoR-gate signals, not review HIGH findings per skill rules

testPlan.gapNotes: INT-S3-02 blocked on load profile documentation dependency

dor.verdictEvalMode: CONDITIONAL PROCEED (H-GOV is process gate, not constraint propagation failure)

evalRunResult.verdict: CPF threshold met (1.0 ≥ 0.80); all 5 canonical constraints propagated through all 5 skills
